title: Smoky Roasted Middle-Eastern Dried White Beans Tagine
description: A hearty, spicy, and aromatic Middle-Eastern stew featuring smoky roasted dried white beans, perfect for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: Dried White Beans (such as Cannellini or Great Northern)

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the dried white beans, 1 cup of water, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Let it soak for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked white beans, then transfer them to a large baking dish or Dutch oven.
In a pan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until it's softened and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the sliced garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan with the onion and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour the prepared onion and spice mixture over the white beans in the baking dish, then add the chopped dried apricots.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 45 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the beans are tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the baking dish from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
